channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: EU warns of stagflation and fiscal crisis risks from Iran war energy shock
item.description: European officials say the surge in energy prices caused by the Iran war has created a stagflationary trend, warning governments against generous fiscal support that could trigger a sovereign debt crisis or higher ECB rates.

item.title: NATO chief welcomes US deployment of 5,000 troops to Poland
item.description: President Trump announced the deployment ahead of a NATO foreign ministers meeting, though the move has created uncertainty among European allies about US strategic priorities.

item.title: Russia sent more nuclear warheads to Belarus in risky escalation
item.description: Russia deployed additional nuclear warheads to Belarus for the first time during joint drills, with analysts calling it a risky move for Minsk amid the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: Gang violence kills at least 25 in Honduras
item.description: Gunmen carried out two separate attacks in Honduras as the government continues a crackdown on organized crime, killing at least 25 people including 6 police officers.

item.title: Alberta to hold referendum on whether to remain in Canada
item.description: Voters in the western province will decide next October whether they want to stay in Canada or move ahead with a binding independence referendum process.

item.title: Russia Falters in Ukraine as Economy Weakens and Casualties Mount
item.description: Analysis examines Russia's struggling war effort in Ukraine amid economic pressures and mounting military losses.

item.title: India Opens $9 Billion Island Megaport Near Strait of Malacca
item.description: India's massive new port project sharpens Beijing's strategic calculations about energy supply routes through the critical waterway.

item.title: Turkish Court Removes Opposition Leader in Major Blow to Democracy
item.description: The appeal court declared Özgür Özel's party leadership victory void, further cementing President Erdoğan's grip on power.

item.title: Netherlands Bans Imports from Israeli Settlements
item.description: The Netherlands becomes the latest European country to block trade with Israeli settlements in occupied territories.

item.title: Japan Core Inflation Hits 4-Year Low Before Energy Shock
item.description: Japan's core inflation fell to a four-year low, though analysts expect a rebound as energy price impacts from the Iran conflict hit the economy.

item.title: Israel Deports 37 French Gaza Flotilla Activists to Turkey
item.description: Israel deported 37 French nationals who attempted to breach the Gaza blockade by sea, sending them to Turkey according to French officials.

item.title: China Denies Forcing Tech Firms to Reject Foreign Investment
item.description: China's powerful state planner said the government has never required Chinese technology companies to reject foreign investment.

item.title: Greek Terror Group Leader Released at 82 After Decades in Prison
item.description: The leader of the Revolutionary Organization 17 November was released from prison at age 82 after serving decades for terrorist attacks.

item.title: Texas Sues Meta and WhatsApp Over Encryption Privacy Claims
item.description: Texas sued WhatsApp alleging the company misled consumers about the strength and scope of its encryption measures.

item.title: WHO Africa Head Warns Ebola Outbreak Risk Should Not Be Underestimated
item.description: The WHO regional director for Africa warned against underestimating the risk of Ebola spread as the outbreak in Congo involves a rare species of the virus.

item.title: UAE Considered Leaving OPEC for Three Years Over Oil Strategy
item.description: A UAE presidential adviser revealed the country considered leaving OPEC for three years over differences on oil production strategy.

item.title: UN Refugee Agency Loses $1 Million in Aid After Russian Strike on Ukraine Warehouse
item.description: The UN refugee agency said $1 million worth of humanitarian aid was lost after a Russian strike hit its warehouse in Ukraine.

item.title: US Pauses $14 Billion Taiwan Arms Sale Citing Iran War Stockpile Needs
item.description: The US Navy said the $14 billion arms sale to Taiwan was paused to ensure munitions stockpiles are available for the Iran conflict.

item.title: Venezuelan Oil Emerges as India's Third-Largest Supplier Amid Hormuz Crisis
item.description: Venezuelan Acting President Delcy Rodriguez is expected to travel to India next week to discuss expanded oil sales as the Hormuz crisis disrupts traditional supply routes.

item.title: Saudi Oil Exports Surge 37% as Kingdom Bypasses Hormuz via Red Sea Route
item.description: Saudi Arabia has dramatically increased oil exports through Red Sea terminals, avoiding the Strait of Hormuz disruption.

item.title: Taliban Decree Formalizes Child Marriage Rules, UN Expresses Grave Concern
item.description: The UN expressed grave concern over a new Taliban law that includes provisions formalizing child marriage rules.

item.title: UAE Official Sees 50-50 Chance for Hormuz Deal, Warns Iran Against Overplaying Hand
item.description: A UAE official put the chances of a Hormuz ceasefire deal at 50-50, warning Iran against overplaying its hand in negotiations.

item.title: Pakistan Mediates US-Iran Peace Talks Amid Uranium and Hormuz Disputes
item.description: Pakistan is seeking a breakthrough in US-Iran peace talks as negotiations continue over uranium enrichment and the Strait of Hormuz crisis.

item.title: US-China Thaw Plants Seeds for Taiwan Flashpoint
item.description: President Trump says a pending $14 billion arms deal with Taiwan is a negotiating chip with Beijing, even as Xi ramps up military and diplomatic pressure on the island.

item.title: NATO Allies Confused by Trump Sudden Shift on European Troop Deployments
item.description: European allies are bewildered by Trump's about-face on US troop movements in Europe, with the sudden policy shifts creating strategic uncertainty.
